semanticCore: Milk, brick, white fluid, suckling, or building blocks
quranicRelevance: Milk for drinking (Laban - 'labanan khalisan', 'anharun min labanin').

keyDerivations.meaning: Milk, white nutritious fluid produced by female mammals, or dairy

figurativeOrRareUsages: وَإِنَّ لَكُمْ فِي الْأَنْعَامِ لَعِبْرَةً ۖ نُسْقِيكُمْ مِمَّا فِي بُطُونِهِ مِنْ بَيْنِ فَرْثٍ وَدَمٍ لَبَنًا خَالِصًا سَائِغًا لِلشَّارِبِينَ (16:66): And indeed, for you in grazing livestock is a lesson. We give you drink from what is in their bellies - between digested food and blood - pure milk, pleasant to the drinkers.
discardedNoise: Clay bricks (Liben) used in historic mudbrick houses.
summaryEn: The root refers to milk (Laban), celebrating the biological extraction of pure, delicious milk from cattle and describing rivers of milk in Paradise.
summaryAr: يشير هذا الجذر إلى الحليب واللبن السائل المغذي (لبن)، واصفاً إعجاز استخلاص اللبن الخالص السائغ من بين الفرث والدم، وأنهار ألبان الجنة.
rawText: بَنَاتُ لَبَنٍ [app. The small guts or intestines, in which originate the lacteals;] the intestines in which is the milk. (M, K.) See حَويَّةٌ, termed بَنَاتُ اللَّبَنِ. === لَبَنَةٌ [n. un. of لَبَنٌ]. (Az, in TA, art. خرس.) عَسَلُ اللُّبْنَى i. q. المَيْعَةُ [now applied to Storax, or styrax] sometimes used for fumigation. (TA.) See art. عسل. لَبَنِيَّةٌ Food made with milk: so in modern Arabic: see خَطِيفَةٌ. لَبُونٌ : see لَقُوحٌ and بَكْرٌ. === إِبْنُ لَبُونٍ A male camel that has entered upon his third year: (S, Mgh, K:) or entering upon his third year: (Msb:) or in his second year. (K.) لَبِنٌ Bricks; (T, S, M, Mgh, Msb, K;) crude, or unburnt, bricks. (MA.) لُبَانٌ [The frankincense-tree] is a tree of the kind called عِضَاه, having a fruit resembling the pistachio-nut, and a resin like the كُنْدُر, [which is said in the S and TA to be the same as the لُبَان,] when it concretes: (O and TA in art. سيع:) it is also, and more commonly, applied to the resin itself, i. e. frankincense, or olibanum: the tree that produces it is now known to be of the genus Boswellia, found in Hadramowt and other parts of Southern Arabia, and also in the opposite (eastern) region of Africa, and in India: it was formerly erroneously supposed to be the Juniperus Lycia. === حَصَى لُبَانٍ: see K, voce عَسَلٌ; and see art. حصى. لُبَانَةٌ مَغْرِبِيَّةٌ : see فَرْبَيُونٌ. لُبَيْنَةٌ [A little milk: dim. of لَبَنَةٌ, n. un. of لَبَنٌ]: see رَثَأَ. لِبَانٌ The sucking of milk or of the breast: (S, Msb, K:) see an ex. in a verse of El-Aashà cited voce أَسْحَمُ: and see 1 in art. غذو. لِبْنَةٌ : see بَنِيقَةٌ. مِلْبَنٌ A thing like the مِحْمَل, upon which bricks (لَبِن) are carried from place to place. (M.) See فَتْخَآءُ.
